name: Autopoiesis Culinary Science Principle
mechanism: Autopoiesis in systems theory operates through the interaction of self-producing system as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through internal processes regenerate components, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—boundary maintenance—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by organizational closure. This process is bounded by membrane separating system from environment, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to culinary science, the mechanism from systems theory suggests that the core units of culinary science undergo an analogous evolutionary process. The variation mechanism (internal processes regenerate components) maps onto the generative processes in culinary science, while the selection pressure (boundary maintenance) corresponds to the evaluative forces that determine which innovations, strategies, or structures persist in the target domain. This cross-domain mapping predicts that culinary science systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in systems theory, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (membrane separating system from environment) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in culinary science designed using systems theory structural principles will show 25% reduction in failure rates compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=60 cases over a 8-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

mechanism: Law and Economics in legal theory operates through the interaction of legal rule as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through alternative rule configurations, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. This process is bounded by Coasian assumptions, transaction costs, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.

prediction: Systems in systems biology designed using legal theory structural principles will show 20% higher performance scores compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=60 cases over a 24-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Squeeze Passage Body Biochemistry Model
mechanism: Squeeze Passage Body Mechanics Optimization in caving operates through the interaction of caver body cross-section at the tightest restriction point as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through shoulder rotation angle, lung inflation volume, arm position (superman vs. arms-back), clothing compression, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—the configuration that minimizes effective cross-section while preserving ability to generate forward thrust wins passage—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by caver transits the restriction without becoming stuck or requiring rescue assistance. This process is bounded by does not apply to vertical squeezes where gravity assists or resists; assumes dry rock without water sumps, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.

This cross-domain mapping predicts that biochemistry systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in caving, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (does not apply to vertical squeezes where gravity assists or resists; assumes dry rock without water sumps) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in biochemistry designed using caving structural principles will show 18% greater diversity indices compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=40 cases over a 18-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Queen Pheromone Colony Cohesion Soap Bubble Physics Strategy
mechanism: Queen Pheromone Colony Cohesion in beekeeping / apiculture operates through the interaction of queen mandibular pheromone molecule distributed across the colony as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through pheromone concentration gradient from queen to periphery of hive, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—workers below a pheromone threshold begin rearing emergency queen cells—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by colony unity and suppression of worker reproduction. This process is bounded by colony size, queen vitality, comb geometry affecting pheromone diffusion, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.

The boundary conditions (colony size, queen vitality, comb geometry affecting pheromone diffusion) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in soap bubble physics designed using beekeeping / apiculture structural principles will show 22% lower error rates compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=120 cases over a 18-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Convolution Impulse Response Shaping Category Theory Theory
mechanism: Convolution Impulse Response Shaping in signal processing operates through the interaction of sample-by-sample multiply-accumulate operation between input and kernel as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through kernel length, coefficient values, and symmetry properties produce different filtering behaviors (lowpass, highpass, bandpass), creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—kernel designs minimizing passband ripple and maximizing stopband attenuation for given length dominate filter design practice—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by frequency response conformance to design specification measured by maximum deviation from target magnitude. This process is bounded by finite kernel length limits frequency selectivity, latency from kernel length, Gibbs phenomenon at sharp transitions, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to category theory, the mechanism from signal processing suggests that the core units of category theory undergo an analogous evolutionary process. The variation mechanism (kernel length, coefficient values, and symmetry properties produce different filtering behaviors (lowpass, highpass, bandpass)) maps onto the generative processes in category theory, while the selection pressure (kernel designs minimizing passband ripple and maximizing stopband attenuation for given length dominate filter design practice) corresponds to the evaluative forces that determine which innovations, strategies, or structures persist in the target domain. This cross-domain mapping predicts that category theory systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in signal processing, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (finite kernel length limits frequency selectivity, latency from kernel length, Gibbs phenomenon at sharp transitions) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in category theory designed using signal processing structural principles will show 12% reduction in resource waste compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=40 cases over a 8-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Minimum Entropy Production Mycology Theory
mechanism: Minimum Entropy Production (Near-Equilibrium) in non-equilibrium thermodynamics operates through the interaction of thermodynamic steady state as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through candidate flux configurations near equilibrium, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—system relaxes to state of minimum entropy production rate—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by entropy production rate (minimized). This process is bounded by linear regime, fixed boundary conditions, Onsager symmetry, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to mycology, the mechanism from non-equilibrium thermodynamics suggests that the core units of mycology undergo an analogous evolutionary process. prediction: Systems in mycology designed using non-equilibrium thermodynamics structural principles will show 30% increase in adaptive capacity compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=100 cases over a 8-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Lava Flow Channelization Systems Theory Principle
mechanism: Lava Flow Channelization in volcanology operates through the interaction of active lava lobe as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through effusion rate fluctuations, slope changes, and cooling-crust formation create competing flow paths, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—paths with highest flux maintain thermal viability; starved branches crust over and solidify—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by flow length achieved before solidification. This process is bounded by effusion rate, ground slope, ambient temperature, lava viscosity, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.

The boundary conditions (effusion rate, ground slope, ambient temperature, lava viscosity) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in systems theory designed using volcanology structural principles will show 40% faster convergence rates compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=80 cases over a 18-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Quasispecies Theory Architecture Algebra
mechanism: Quasispecies Theory (Eigen) in virology operates through the interaction of RNA sequence variant as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through high mutation rate during replication, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—selection acts on mutant cloud distribution, not single sequence—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by mean fitness of quasispecies ensemble. This process is bounded by error threshold: mutation rate must stay below inverse of genome length, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to architecture, the mechanism from virology suggests that the core units of architecture undergo an analogous evolutionary process. This cross-domain mapping predicts that architecture systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in virology, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (error threshold: mutation rate must stay below inverse of genome length) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in architecture designed using virology structural principles will show 35% improvement in resilience measures compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=200 cases over a 6-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Pest Dilution Effect Macroeconomics Architecture
mechanism: Pest Dilution Effect in agroecology operates through the interaction of herbivore-host encounter in a diverse field as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through crop diversity, spatial arrangement, non-host plant density, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—specialist herbivores have lower colonization success in diverse stands—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by inverse pest load per unit crop yield. This process is bounded by pest mobility, host specificity, field scale, natural enemy abundance, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.

The boundary conditions (pest mobility, host specificity, field scale, natural enemy abundance) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in macroeconomics designed using agroecology structural principles will show 22% lower error rates compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=200 cases over a 6-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.
